article_title: Automotive Grade Linux Ships First Open Source SoDeV Reference Platform for Software-Defined Vehicles, Adds Five New Members
summary: Substantively accurate AGL SoDeV coverage; one source misattribution (support-cadence sentence cited to ATTI but actually carried by the LF/AGL/PRNewswire releases) filed as a correction.

findings.message: The sentence 'The "Ultimate Unagi" code base will be supported for the next two years, with updates released approximately three weeks after each Scarthgap release by the Yocto Project' is attributed to Automotive Testing Technology International, but that text does not appear in the ATTI snapshot (source-3). The exact wording appears verbatim in the Linux Foundation (source-0), Automotive Grade Linux (source-1), and PR Newswire (source-2) snapshots — all of which are already in article.sources.
findings.details: Recoverable: the fact is accurate and triple-sourced among the article's own cited releases. Filed as a corrections record re-attributing the claim. Not a fabrication.

content_preview.title: Automotive Grade Linux Ships First Open Source SoDeV Reference Platform for Software-Defined Vehicles, Adds Five New Members
content_preview.summary: AGL released the initial SoDeV reference platform in its 'Ultimate Unagi' code base on May 14, 2026, bundling Xen, Zephyr, and Linux containers to decouple vehicle software from hardware.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Automotive Grade Linux (AGL), a collaborative open source project hosted by the Linux Foundation, has released the first version of its SoDeV reference platform for software-defined vehicles and welcomed five new members. The initial release shipped on May 14, 2026, inside the latest AGL Unified Code Base, codenamed "Ultimate Unagi," according to the [Linux Foundation](https://www.linuxfoundation.org/press/automotive-grade-linux-releases-open-source-sodev-reference-platform-for-soft...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-organized News piece (579 words). Accurately frames SoDeV as a pre-integrated Yocto-based reference stack for software-defined vehicles.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 snapshots read from disk. source-0 (Linux Foundation) confirms 'Ultimate Unagi', the May 14 2026 release, Scarthgap LTS 5.0.16, the 'decouple software development from hardware availability' quote, Sparrow Hawk boards, and the Panasonic/Honda/Toyota/Mazda/AISIN/Renesas contributor list. source-1 (automotivelinux.org) confirms the five new members (EMQ, Lineo Solutions, MediaTek, VA Linux Systems Japan, Very Good Ventures) and the Flutter UI detail. source-3 (ATTI) confirms the Xen/VirtIO/Zephyr bundle and the Dan Cauchy 'distinct and valuable expertise' welcome quote. source-2 (PRNewswire) confirms the Cauchy 'breadth of innovation that open source collaboration makes possible' quote.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics (codename, date, Yocto version, board, member names) trace to snapshots and are accurate. One source-attribution error: the two-year-support / three-week-update-cadence sentence is cited to ATTI but the verbatim text is actually in the LF/AGL/PRNewswire releases (all already cited in the article). Recoverable via correction — the fact itself is accurate and well-sourced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; Analysis section clearly labeled.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior AGL or SoDeV coverage in the archive.
concerns: One misattributed (but accurate and otherwise-sourced) sentence — filed as a corrections record rather than a rejection.
recommendations: Added automotivelinux.org and automotivetestingtechnologyinternational.com to the allowlist as the AGL primary source and an automotive trade publication.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate and fully sourced. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS for the single source misattribution.
